q: Who was the first person created by God?
e: Genesis 2:7 — God formed man from the dust of the ground.

q: Out of what was Eve created?
e: Genesis 2:21-22 — God took one of Adam's ribs and made it into a woman.

q: What was the first thing God created?
e: Genesis 1:3 — God said, 'Let there be light.'

q: How many days did God take to create the world?
e: Genesis 1:31-2:2 — God finished His work on the sixth day and rested on the seventh.

q: Who was the oldest man mentioned in Genesis?
e: Genesis 5:27 — Methuselah lived 969 years.

q: Which son of Adam killed his brother?
e: Genesis 4:8 — Cain killed his brother Abel.

q: What was the sign of God's covenant with Noah?
e: Genesis 9:13 — God set His rainbow in the clouds as a sign of the covenant.

q: How many people were on the ark?
e: Genesis 7:13 — Noah, his wife, his three sons, and their wives entered the ark.

q: What was the name of the tower built to reach heaven?
e: Genesis 11:4-9 — The people tried to build a tower to the heavens, so God confused their language.

q: Who was the father of the twelve tribes of Israel?
e: Genesis 35:23-26 — Jacob, also named Israel, had twelve sons who became the tribes.

q: Who was Isaac's wife?
e: Genesis 24:67 — Isaac married Rebekah and loved her.

q: What did Esau sell his birthright for?
e: Genesis 25:34 — Esau sold his birthright for bread and lentil stew.

q: What new name did God give Jacob?
e: Genesis 32:28 — Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel.

q: Which of Jacob's sons was sold into slavery?
e: Genesis 37:28 — Joseph's brothers sold him to Midianite traders.

q: Who was Joseph's father?
e: Genesis 37:3 — Now Israel (Jacob) loved Joseph more than any of his other sons.

q: Who tempted Eve in the Garden of Eden?
e: Genesis 3:1 — The serpent was more crafty than any of the wild animals.

q: What was the first bird Noah sent out from the ark?
e: Genesis 8:7 — He sent out a raven, and it kept flying back and forth.

q: Who was Sarah's maidservant who had a son with Abraham?
e: Genesis 16:1-3 — Sarai gave her Egyptian maidservant Hagar to Abram.

q: What was the name of Hagar's son?
e: Genesis 16:15 — Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram named him Ishmael.

q: What city was Lot living in when it was destroyed by fire?
e: Genesis 19:24-25 — The Lord rained down burning sulfur on Sodom and Gomorrah.

q: What happened to Lot's wife when she looked back?
o: She turned into a pillar of salt
e: Genesis 19:26 — Lot's wife looked back and became a pillar of salt.

q: How old was Abraham when Isaac was born?
e: Genesis 21:5 — Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born.

q: Whom did God tell Abraham to sacrifice on a mountain?
e: Genesis 22:2 — God said, 'Take your son, your only son Isaac... and sacrifice him.'

q: What did Jacob see in his dream at Bethel?
e: Genesis 28:12 — He had a dream in which he saw a stairway (ladder) resting on the earth.

q: Which son of Jacob was the oldest?
e: Genesis 29:32 — Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben.

q: Who was Jacob's favorite wife?
e: Genesis 29:30 — Jacob loved Rachel more than Leah.

q: How many years did Jacob work for Laban to marry Rachel?
e: Genesis 29:18-30 — Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and then another seven.

q: What was the name of the place where Jacob wrestled with God?
e: Genesis 32:30 — Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, 'I saw God face to face.'

q: Who bought Joseph as a slave in Egypt?
e: Genesis 39:1 — Potiphar, an Egyptian who was one of Pharaoh’s officials, bought him.

q: Whose dream did Joseph interpret while in prison?
e: Genesis 40:5-19 — Joseph interpreted the dreams of the cupbearer and the baker.

q: How many years of famine did Joseph predict?
e: Genesis 41:29-30 — Seven years of great abundance will come, followed by seven years of famine.

q: Which brother stayed behind in prison while the others returned to Canaan?
e: Genesis 42:24 — Joseph took Simeon from them and had him bound before their eyes.

q: Which brother offered to take Benjamin's place as Joseph's slave?
e: Genesis 44:33 — Judah offered to stay as Joseph's slave instead of the boy Benjamin.

q: In what land did the Israelites settle in Egypt?
e: Genesis 47:27 — Now the Israelites settled in Egypt in the region of Goshen.

q: Who was the first woman?
e: Genesis 3:20 — Adam named his wife Eve, because she would be the mother of all the living.

q: What was the name of the garden where Adam and Eve lived?
e: Genesis 2:8 — Now the Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden.

q: On what day did God create the sun, moon, and stars?
e: Genesis 1:14-19 — God made two great lights and the stars on the fourth day.

q: Who walked with God and then was no more because God took him?
e: Genesis 5:24 — Enoch walked faithfully with God; then he was no more, because God took him away.

q: What kind of wood was used to build the ark?
e: Genesis 6:14 — So make yourself an ark of cypress (gopher) wood.

q: Where did the ark come to rest?
e: Genesis 8:4 — The ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.

q: Who was Abraham's father?
e: Genesis 11:27 — Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran.

q: Who was Lot's uncle?
e: Genesis 12:5 — Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot...

q: How many righteous people were needed to save Sodom?
e: Genesis 18:32 — God said, 'For the sake of ten, I will not destroy it.'

q: What was the name of Isaac's firstborn son?
e: Genesis 25:25 — The first to come out was red... so they named him Esau.

q: Who was Laban's sister and Isaac's wife?
e: Genesis 24:29 — Rebekah had a brother named Laban.

q: Which brother suggested selling Joseph rather than killing him?
e: Genesis 37:26-27 — Judah said, 'What will we gain if we kill our brother? Let’s sell him.'

q: What did Joseph's brothers dip his coat in to deceive their father?
e: Genesis 37:31 — Then they got Joseph’s robe, slaughtered a goat and dipped the robe in the blood.

q: What was the name of Joseph's wife in Egypt?
e: Genesis 41:45 — Pharaoh gave him Asenath daughter of Poti-Phera... to be his wife.

q: Who were Joseph's two sons?
e: Genesis 41:50-52 — Before the years of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph... Manasseh and Ephraim.

q: How old was Jacob when he died?
e: Genesis 47:28 — Jacob lived in Egypt seventeen years, and the years of his life were 147.
